data.title: Topic-tag substring filter with match-none default on empty tags
data.description:
As a DSO maintainer configuring reference URLs, I can add URLs with or without topic tags and predictably control when they apply to implementation-plan task annotations.

Done definitions:
- Topic-tag filter applies substring match against task title+description
- URL references with empty/absent tags field default to match-none semantics (excluded from filter output); documented as the default in CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md
- URL references with explicit tags use substring matching (any-tag-match semantics when multiple tags present)
- Story ba7d-729d's "sparse corpus" sub-test continues to pass after implementation (verifies crowd-out prevention interaction with tag filter)
- Unit tests cover: empty tags (match-none), single tag match/no-match, multiple tags any-match

Considerations:
- [Correctness] Match-none prevents 12-budget exhaustion on untagged URL references spamming every task
- [Security] Substring match must not allow regex injection — input is treated as literal substring
